name: list_directory_tool
summary: List the entries of a directory with type and size. Use to get a quick overview of an unfamiliar layout before drilling in.
constraints: Returns entries sorted alphabetically by display name.
Each line carries the entry name, its kind (`file` or `dir`), and — for files — its size in bytes.
The `path` is resolved relative to the agent's working directory.
anti_patterns: To find files by pattern across the tree — use `glob_tool`.
To read a single file — use `read_file_tool`.
output: Plain text. One entry per line, alphabetically sorted: `<name>  file  <size_bytes>` for files, `<name>  dir` for directories.
In recursive mode, `<name>` is the path relative to `path`. Empty string for an empty directory.

recursive.description: Walk subdirectories and list every entry beneath `path` (default: false). Use sparingly — prefer `glob_tool` for large trees.
